config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I think society's interests matter a lot, but they should not automatically override individual rights. In my view, rights protect people from being sacrificed for vague claims of the greater good, and once that line is crossed, abuse becomes too easy. A good society should balance both, but individual rights need a strong priority.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Menurut saya, kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu, karena di Amerika saya melihat hak-hak dasar justru ada untuk membatasi kekuasaan mayoritas. Saya tidak percaya pada argumen “demi kebaikan bersama” kalau itu dipakai terlalu mudah untuk membenarkan pelanggaran kebebasan berbicara, privasi, atau proses hukum yang adil. Tentu ada situasi tertentu seperti kesehatan publik atau keamanan umum di mana kepentingan bersama bisa membatasi hak secara sempit dan sementara, tetapi pembatasan itu harus benar-benar perlu dan tidak berlebihan. Kalau tidak, istilah “kepentingan masyarakat” bisa jadi alasan yang sangat berbahaya untuk menekan individu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I reject the idea that society should generally take priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective as someone from Indonesia, I understand why people emphasize harmony, order, and the common good, but I have also seen how easily “for society” can become a convenient excuse for abusing power or silencing weaker people. Public health or security can justify limited restrictions in specific cases, but those limits must be narrow, temporary, and accountable. If individual rights are treated as secondary by default, then the society we are protecting stops being just.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Dari perspektif saya sebagai orang Amerika, hak dasar memang dirancang justru untuk membatasi mayoritas dan pemerintah, karena sejarah menunjukkan bahwa “demi kebaikan bersama” sering dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Saya setuju ada keadaan tertentu seperti kesehatan publik atau keamanan, tetapi pembatasannya harus sempit, sementara, dan benar-benar perlu, bukan dijadikan alasan default. Kalau hak individu selalu bisa dikorbankan demi kepentingan masyarakat, maka perlindungan terhadap orang yang paling rentan akan menjadi sangat lemah.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text:
I reject the statement as a general rule: in my view, society’s interests matter, but they should not sit above individual rights by default. From an Indonesian perspective, I understand the appeal of harmony and collective order, but I have also seen how “for the public good” can be used to silence dissent, excuse corruption, or pressure weaker people who have little power to push back.  

That said, I do not believe rights are absolute in every situation; public health, disaster response, or serious security threats can justify limited restrictions. But those limits must be narrow, temporary, and accountable, otherwise the idea of society becomes a tool for the powerful rather than a protection for everyone.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan itu sebagai prinsip umum: hak individu tidak boleh ditempatkan di bawah kepentingan masyarakat secara default. Dari perspektif Amerika, saya melihat alasan “demi kebaikan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan kekuasaan mayoritas atau pemerintah menekan kebebasan berbicara, privasi, dan proses hukum yang adil. Memang ada keadaan tertentu seperti kesehatan publik atau keamanan yang bisa membenarkan pembatasan, tetapi pembatasan itu harus sempit, sementara, dan benar-benar perlu. Kalau tidak, yang disebut kepentingan masyarakat justru bisa menjadi alat paling mudah untuk mengorbankan orang yang paling rentan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
